title: Instructor Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide to using the Instructor AI tool effectively.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the AI's responses.
commands.scenario: When generating creative writing or brainstorming ideas.

commands.description: Selects the second version of the Instructor model.
commands.scenario: When you need the latest features and improvements in model performance.

commands.description: Simplifies complex topics for better understanding.
commands.scenario: When explaining technical concepts to non-experts or beginners.

commands.description: Limits the response length to save on costs and processing time.
commands.scenario: When generating concise summaries or responses.

commands.description: Enables streaming responses for real-time applications.
commands.scenario: When building a live chat application that requires immediate feedback.

commands.command: "Think step by step. Then verify your answer."
commands.description: Encourages the AI to use logical reasoning and verification.
commands.scenario: When solving complex problems that require detailed analysis.

commands.description: Defines sequences that will terminate the AI's response.
commands.scenario: When you need to control where the response ends, such as in dialogue systems.

commands.description: Generates a balanced view by listing advantages and disadvantages.
commands.scenario: When evaluating options or making decisions.

commands.description: Provides detailed logging output for debugging purposes.
commands.scenario: When troubleshooting issues with the AI's responses or behavior.

commands.description: Reduces the likelihood of repeating the same line verbatim.
commands.scenario: When generating varied and interesting text outputs.

commands.description: Creates a concise summary of a given document or text.
commands.scenario: When needing a quick overview of lengthy content.

commands.description: Sets a timeout for API calls to prevent hanging requests.
commands.scenario: When integrating with systems that require quick responses.

commands.description: Generates detailed instructions for completing a task.
commands.scenario: When creating tutorials or guides for users.

commands.description: Identifies possible risks associated with a specific action.
commands.scenario: When conducting risk assessments or planning projects.

commands.description: Enforce structured output by passing a Pydantic model to the instructor call for type-safe parsing.
Configure automatic retry logic for validation errors or API timeouts to improve system reliability.

commands.command: system_prompt="You are a senior software architect."
commands.description: Define a persistent persona in the system message to set high-level constraints and behavioral context.
Force the underlying LLM to operate in JSON mode to ensure valid syntax before Pydantic validation.
Use the patch function to wrap existing OpenAI/Anthropic clients to inject Instructor's validation logic.
Passes external context into Pydantic validators to perform cross-reference checks during the extraction process.
Uses Markdown-wrapped JSON mode to improve parsing reliability when using models that struggle with raw JSON output.
Implements exponential backoff strategy for handling rate limits (429 errors) more gracefully than simple integer retries.

commands.command: prompt: 'Extract data into the schema. If unsure, return null for that field rather than hallucinating.'
commands.description: A prompt-engineering pattern to minimize hallucinations by explicitly authorizing null values within the Pydantic schema.
Global configuration to set a default retry policy across all instructor-wrapped client calls in a session.
